questions.question: What does a Silhouette score close to 1 indicate about clustering quality?

answers.a: Data points are assigned to incorrect clusters
answers.b: Clusters are compact and well separated
answers.d: All clusters contain equal number of samples

explanations.a: Incorrect cluster assignment lowers the silhouette score.
explanations.b: A value close to 1 means samples are closer to their own cluster than to neighboring clusters.
explanations.d: Cluster size equality is not measured by silhouette score.

questions.question: What does the Davies–Bouldin score measure in clustering evaluation?

answers.a: Trade-off between cluster compactness and separation

explanations.a: The DB index evaluates intra-cluster similarity and inter-cluster separation.
explanations.b: The number of clusters is defined by the algorithm parameter.
explanations.c: It considers both centroid distance and cluster spread.
explanations.d: Clustering is unsupervised so accuracy is not used.

questions.question: If a new feature highly correlated with an existing feature is added to K-Means clustering, what is the most likely outcome?

answers.c: The feature may add redundancy without improving cluster quality

explanations.a: Additional features do not always improve clustering.
explanations.b: Centroids rarely become identical due to correlation.
explanations.c: Redundant features may not provide new information.
explanations.d: WCSS cannot become zero unless all points equal centroids.

questions.question: What does a lower Davies–Bouldin score indicate?

answers.b: Better clustering with compact and well-separated clusters

explanations.a: Overlapping clusters increase the DB score.
explanations.b: Lower values mean clusters are compact and far apart.
explanations.d: DB score measures cluster quality, not cluster count.

questions.question: The Calinski–Harabasz index evaluates clustering based on which principle?

answers.a: Ratio of between-cluster variance to within-cluster variance

explanations.a: A higher ratio indicates well-separated clusters.
explanations.b: CH score does not determine cluster count.
explanations.c: Silhouette score is a separate metric.

questions.question: Why does K-Means minimize the Within-Cluster Sum of Squares (WCSS)?

answers.b: To keep points close to their cluster centroids

explanations.d: K-Means does not specifically remove outliers.

questions.question: Why is feature scaling important before applying K-Means clustering?

answers.a: It reduces the number of clusters
answers.b: It prevents features with larger ranges from dominating distance calculations

explanations.b: K-Means uses distance metrics sensitive to feature magnitude.
explanations.d: Scaling may improve clustering but does not guarantee higher silhouette score.

questions.question: In the Elbow Method, what does the “elbow point” represent?

answers.b: The point where adding more clusters gives diminishing improvement
answers.c: The point where clusters become overlapping

explanations.a: WCSS keeps decreasing as clusters increase.
explanations.b: The elbow indicates the optimal trade-off between cluster count and compactness.
explanations.c: Overlap is unrelated to the elbow concept.
explanations.d: Elbow method uses WCSS, not silhouette score.

questions.question: Which evaluation metric behavior typically indicates overlapping clusters?

explanations.a: High silhouette score indicates good separation.
explanations.b: Low DB score indicates good clustering.
explanations.d: High CH score suggests well-separated clusters.

questions.question: Why is K-Means classified as an unsupervised learning algorithm?

answers.a: It automatically selects the best number of clusters
answers.b: It does not require labeled training data
answers.c: It always finds the global optimal clusters
answers.d: It only works with categorical features

explanations.a: The number of clusters must usually be specified.
explanations.b: Unsupervised learning works without labeled outputs.
explanations.c: K-Means may converge to local optima.
explanations.d: It primarily works with numerical features.
